vulnerability.title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the group owner of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chgrp root /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: newgroup="" if getent group "0" >/dev/null 2>&1; then newgroup="0" fi if [[ -z "${newgroup}" ]]; then >&2 echo "0 is not a defined group on the system" else find -P /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -type d ! -group 0 -exec chgrp --no-dereference "$newgroup" {} \; fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes the group owner of the /var/log directory to root (group ID 0). This is a standard file permission adjustment that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services, and it is reversible. The change is low risk and aligns with typical STIG hardening practices.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Incorrect group ownership could allow non‑privileged users to modify or read log files; Potential audit non‑compliance if group is not root
